frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Vikram: Yaar, itna late kyon karti ho? You know how Mumbai is after a certain hour, right? For a woman alone, it's just not safe. Thoda apna bhi khayal rakho, na. Next time, just try to leave earlier.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, disguised as practical advice and concern, is misogynistic because it places the entire burden of safety on the woman (his wife) by blaming her for being out late and restricting her behavior, rather than acknowledging broader societal issues or male violence. It embodies the 'Advising women not to be out late rather than addressing male violence' surface manifestation.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your wife after she texted you that she is stuck at a friend's place in Andheri and will take an Ola home at midnight because the last local has left. You are telling her why you are angry and what she should have thought about before letting it get this late and what kind of city Mumbai is at midnight for a woman alone in a cab.
